---
title: "prexorctl setup"
description: "Interactive setup flow for PrexorCloud components"
---

<!-- auto-generated by tools/gen-cli-docs.ts — do not edit by hand. -->

Interactive setup flow for PrexorCloud components.
Opens a browser wizard by default (loopback only, 127.0.0.1:9100) that asks
every required field with smart defaults. Headless hosts (no DISPLAY/BROWSER,
containers, CI) automatically fall back to the TTY form. Pass --no-browser to
force TTY mode.
The wizard installs one component at a time: Controller (on the cluster's
control-plane VPS), Daemon (on each worker VPS), or Dashboard (on its own
VPS or alongside the controller). The CLI auto-links to the controller after
install — no separate 'prexorctl login' needed on the same host.
Native installs that provision packages or register systemd units must be
run as root. Compose mode runs as the invoking user.

## Usage

```text
prexorctl setup [flags]
```

## Flags

```text
--browser                              Open a loopback wizard in your default browser. The browser is the default UI; pass --browser=false (or --no-browser) to fall back to the TTY prompts. Headless hosts (no DISPLAY/BROWSER, containers, CI) fall back automatically. (default true)
        --browser-addr string                  host:port the wizard listens on. Empty defaults to 127.0.0.1:9100, or 0.0.0.0:9100 when --public is set.
        --browser-idle-timeout duration        Auto-shutdown the wizard after this much inactivity. Empty = 30m default.
        --browser-open                         Try to launch the system default browser at the wizard URL. Disable on headless hosts. (default true)
        --component string                     Component to install in non-interactive mode: controller or daemon
        --controller-cors-origin string        Controller dashboard CORS origin in non-interactive mode
        --controller-grpc-port string          Controller gRPC port in non-interactive mode
        --controller-http-port string          Controller HTTP port in non-interactive mode
        --controller-install-dir string        Controller install directory (default "/opt/prexorcloud/controller")
        --controller-mongo-mode string         Controller MongoDB source in non-interactive mode: local or remote
        --controller-mongo-uri string          Controller MongoDB URI for remote MongoDB in non-interactive mode
        --controller-redis-mode string         Controller Redis source in non-interactive mode: local or remote
        --controller-redis-uri string          Controller Redis URI for remote Redis in non-interactive mode
        --daemon-controller-grpc-port string   Daemon controller gRPC port in non-interactive mode
        --daemon-controller-host string        Daemon controller host in non-interactive mode
        --daemon-controller-http-port string   Daemon controller HTTP port (for join-token redemption) in non-interactive mode; default 8080
        --daemon-install-dir string            Daemon install directory (default "/opt/prexorcloud/daemon")
        --daemon-join-token string             Daemon join token in non-interactive mode
        --daemon-node-id string                Daemon node ID in non-interactive mode
        --dashboard-admin-password string      Controller admin password (used once to register CORS origin, then discarded)
        --dashboard-admin-user string          Controller admin username (default "admin")
        --dashboard-controller-url string      Controller base URL (e.g. https://controller.example.com:8080)
        --dashboard-install-dir string         Dashboard install directory (default "/opt/prexorcloud/dashboard")
        --dashboard-listen-port string         Local port the dashboard listens on (default "80")
        --dashboard-public-url string          Public URL the dashboard will be served at (e.g. https://dash.example.com)
        --dashboard-serve-mode string          How to serve the bundle: nginx | systemd-nginx | behind-existing-proxy (default "nginx")
        --dashboard-tls-email string           ACME registration email (letsencrypt mode only)
        --dashboard-tls-mode string            TLS mode: none | letsencrypt | custom | terminated-upstream (default "none")
    -h, --help                                 help for setup
        --install-mode string                  Install mode: native or compose
        --manage-firewall                      In --public mode: try to open the wizard's port via ufw / firewall-cmd / iptables, and remove the rule on shutdown. Disable when you'd rather manage the firewall yourself or you're behind a cloud-provider firewall that prexorctl can't see. (default true)
        --no-browser                           Force the TTY prompt flow instead of the browser wizard. Shorthand for --browser=false.
        --non-interactive                      Run setup without prompts using flags and defaults
        --public                               Not recommended unless --ssh-tunnel doesn't fit. Bind the wizard to a non-loopback address with TLS + token auth enabled, so you can paste a token-protected URL into a remote browser. Triggers a self-signed-cert warning unless you front it with a trusted certificate, and exposes the wizard's port to anyone who can reach the host during the setup window.
        --public-host string                   Hostname or IP printed in the wizard URL when --public is set. Defaults to the first non-loopback IPv4 detected on this host. Override when the machine sits behind NAT and you want the URL to use a public DNS name.
        --service-mode string                  Service registration mode: prompt, enable, or disable
        --ssh-tunnel ssh -L                    Recommended for remote VPSes. Bind 127.0.0.1 (no TLS, no browser warning) and print the laptop-side ssh -L command to run — the wizard traffic rides your existing SSH tunnel. Auto-enabled when $SSH_CONNECTION is set on a headless box. Overrides --public.
        --startup-validation-mode string       Startup validation mode after native controller service registration: prompt, enable, or disable
```

## Global flags

```text
--ascii               Use ASCII glyphs only (no unicode box drawing, sparklines, etc.)
        --context string      Override active context for this invocation
    -c, --controller string   Override controller URL
    -j, --json                Output in JSON format
        --no-color            Disable colored output
    -t, --token string        Override auth token
    -v, --verbose             Show HTTP request/response details
```
